item.title: The Marshall Project: Diversity, Equity and Inclusion, 2025
item.description: Our ninth annual diversity report notes developments in 2025 and lays out goals for the year ahead.

item.title: Journalists: New Data to Investigate the Consequences of Hospital Drug Testing at Childbirth
item.description: We collected child welfare data in 21 states to report on the consequences of faulty drug tests for pregnant women, including referrals to law enforce...

item.title: After Disputed Idaho Raid, Lawsuit Tries Different Approach to Hold ICE Accountable
item.description: Itâs nearly impossible to sue federal officials for civil rights violations, but a new ACLU filing could become a test case for how to do it.

item.title: How We Counted Referrals to Police About Substance Use During Pregnancy
item.description: New analysis finds the parents of 70,000 newborns were reported to law enforcement over substance use allegations, often based on flawed drug tests.

item.title: Tens of Thousands of Mothers Were Flagged to Police Over Flawed Drug Tests at Childbirth
item.description: The Marshall Project found more than 70,000 cases referred to law enforcement over allegations of substance use during pregnancy â and thatâs a signif...
